stem: A IT operations data backup uses 3 parallel storage devices for redundancy. The data recoverable requires at least one storage device to function. storage device 1 has a reliability of 89%. storage device 2 has a reliability of 91%. storage device 3 has a reliability between 62% and 75% (uncertain due to limited operational data). Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all storage devices simultaneously, estimated between 2.0% and 4.6%. What is the overall probability that the data recoverable?

ground_truth.answer: P(data recoverable) ranges from 0.9504 to 0.9776. Point estimate: 0.964.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 storage devices fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.11 × 0.09 × 0.38 = 0.0038. P(works) = 0.9962.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.11 × 0.09 × 0.25 = 0.0025. P(works) = 0.9975.
With common-cause failure (2.0%-4.6%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9504, 0.9776].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
